invented_voltage_level.evidence: All voltage values cited (+24V, +10V, 30VDC/30mA, 48VDC/50mA, 250VAC/30VDC relay ratings) match the printed specs next to DO, DO1/DO2, and R1O/R1C/R1 blocks; the response explicitly states the mains/DC-bus voltage is 'not printed' rather than inventing one.

invented_device_tag.evidence: All tags used (MC, Safety PLC, E STOP, 5KΩ pot, R1O/R1C/R1, STO1/STO2, SCM, AO1/ACM, SG+/SG-/SGND) appear verbatim on the drawing.

incorrect_terminal_or_destination_as_fac.evidence: Terminal-to-function mapping (DI1/FWD, DI2/REV, DI3-DI6 multi-step speed, DI7 pulse input, DCM common, AI1/AI2/ACM/+10V, DO/DO1/DO2/DOC, AO1/ACM, STO1/STO2/SCM, SG+/SG-/SGND) all correspond to labels visible in the diagram.

missed_hazardous_voltage_clearly_shown.evidence: Response explicitly warns about the DC bus retained charge, mains input, and relay contacts carrying up to 250VAC even when drive appears off, consistent with the 3.0A/5.0A@250VAC/30VDC relay rating block shown.

false_certainty_on_illegible_area.evidence: The 'Couldn't confirm' section appropriately flags ambiguous items like the duplicate R1C/R1 relay depiction and NPN/PNP jumper detail rather than asserting them as fact.

non_electrical_image_treated_as_print.evidence: Image is a legitimate electrical schematic (Full I/O Wiring Diagram) and was treated as such throughout.

sheet_identity.note: Correctly identifies 'Full I/O Wiring Diagram,' page 2-38, Chapter 2, DURApulse GS20/GS20X — matches title block exactly.

circuit_purpose.note: Accurately summarizes power path (breaker→drive→motor), DC bus with optional reactor/brake resistor, and control/safety I/O sections, all visibly present.

device_identification.note: MC is described as a standard 'main contactor' inline in the summary ('passes through a circuit breaker/fuse and a main contactor (MC)') though the drawing's gray box and NOTE frame it as a recommended optional protective circuit, not standard equipment.

wire_cable_identification.note: Response defers wire/cable detail with '🔗 Wires / cables … (truncated — reply "map" for detail)', leaving this section largely unaddressed despite shielded-cable symbols shown in the legend.

terminal_mapping.note: Exhaustive terminal list (DI1-DI7, DCM, AI1/AI2/ACM/+10V, DO/DO1/DO2/DOC, AO1/ACM, R1O/R1C/R1, STO1/STO2/SCM, SG+/SG-/SGND) matches drawing labels precisely.

voltage_identification.note: Correctly cites printed values (30VDC/30mA 33kHz, 48VDC/50mA, 250VAC/30VDC relay ratings, +24V, +10V) without inventing the unspecified mains voltage.

cross_reference_accuracy.note: Correctly notes the R1C/R1 terminals appear twice (near input section and in the multi-function relay output block) and flags the duplication as unresolved — a real feature of the drawing.

uncertainty_calibration.note: Appropriately hedges on relay duplication, NPN/PNP jumper details, and grounding of DCM/ACM commons rather than asserting unclear details as fact.

unsupported_or_invented_claims.note: Mostly grounded in visible content; main soft issue is treating MC as a standard main contactor rather than the drawing's 'recommended' protective circuit per the NOTE box.

technician_usefulness.note: Provides clear operational summary, signal/device breakdown, and actionable safety guidance suitable for a technician reviewing this print.

safety_language.note: Explicitly warns about lethal DC bus charge, lockout/verification before touching motor/DC leads, and live relay contacts up to 250VAC — consistent with ratings printed on the sheet.

readability.note: Well-structured with headers and bullets, though emoji-heavy formatting is stylistic rather than substantive.

response_completeness.note: Both the initial response and the 'map' follow-up defer content ('… (more — reply "map")' and '… (truncated — reply "map" for detail)'), leaving device and wire lists incomplete.

map_offer_supported.note: The map follow-up delivers accurate signal and device detail as promised, but the wires/cables section is truncated again, only partially fulfilling the offer.

verified_strengths: Terminal labels (DI1-DI7, AI1/AI2, AO1/ACM, DO/DO1/DO2/DOC, R1O/R1C/R1, STO1/STO2/SCM, SG+/SG-/SGND) all match the drawing exactly.
Voltage/current ratings cited (30VDC/30mA, 48VDC/50mA, 250VAC/30VDC relay specs, +24V, +10V) are directly read from the printed spec blocks.
Correctly flags the R1C/R1 terminal appearing in two places on the sheet as an open question rather than asserting equivalence outright.
Safety commentary about DC bus residual charge and STO function is well-grounded and appropriately cautious.

suspected_errors_or_hallucinations.claim: "Incoming one- or three-phase power passes through a circuit breaker/fuse and a main contactor (MC) into the drive input terminals"
suspected_errors_or_hallucinations.why: The drawing's gray dashed box and accompanying NOTE describe the MC/R1C-R1 circuit as a recommended optional protective addition ('It is recommended that you install a protective circuit...'), not a standard main contactor always present in the power path.

items_requiring_technician_review: Confirm whether the MC/protective circuit shown in the gray box is installed in the actual field wiring, since the print marks it as a recommendation, not a mandatory component.
Verify NPN vs PNP jumper position physically, since the diagram shows only a small symbol and the response could not confirm details from the image.
Complete the wires/cables listing and shielded-cable routing that were deferred in both responses.
summary: The response is accurate and well-grounded against the visible terminal labels, voltage/current ratings, and functional blocks on the GS20/GS20X Full I/O Wiring Diagram, with no invented tags or voltages and good uncertainty calibration on ambiguous areas like the duplicated R1C/R1 relay. Minor deductions stem from characterizing the recommended MC protective circuit as a standard main contactor and from repeatedly deferring wire/cable details to a further 'map' reply that was itself truncated, leaving completeness incomplete.
